passages.title: New perspectives on food production
passages.source_html:
New perspectives on food production


Solving the food problem requires scientific progress, but also an understanding of social context and cultures


Is new technology needed for global food production?


A 


The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations notes that domestic prices of staple food are continuing to increase, leading to a rise in the number of people worldwide who are chronically underfed. According to Erik Millstone, a food and agriculture researcher funded by the Economic and Social Research Council (ESRC), the natural science community tends to view world hunger as if it were a problem that could be solved simply by increasing total production. But, he says, ‘Most people who are chronically hungry are so not because of the scarcity of food but because they are unable to afford what is available. In addition, the food trade is so globalised that food is often exported from areas where people are hungry and sent to countries where people already have sufficient.’


B 


Millstone believes the problem can only be solved by changing the conditions for poor subsistence farmers and providing the support they need to grow more food. And that is not enough on its own – they also have to have facilities for storing it so that their food can be kept safe and in good condition until it is needed. But, he adds, increasing productivity through technology is not the answer. An example is the new genetically-engineered varieties of high-performance maize. The problem with these new varieties is that if you save seed and plant it again next year, its vigour has diminished. Most North American and European farmers can afford to get new seed every year; ‘Poor farmers cannot, so they need traditional varieties whose seed can be saved and replanted. Giving farmers access to credit also doesn’t help; it adds risk. Poor farmers should not be thought of as entrepreneurs looking to invest their money. They are looking to diminish their risk.’ Ultimately, he concludes, ‘Instead of devoting resources to research for intensifying commercial farming, we should devote them to enhancing the techniques available to subsistence farmers, and to developing appropriate tools for them to use, because their need is the greatest. Increasing their productivity will do more to enhance food security for those who are hungry than anything else we can do.’


Looking for local solutions in the UK


C 


In contrast to Millstone, Gareth Edwards Jones, a professor of agriculture and land use at the University of Bangor in Wales, focuses on food production in the UK. In recent years ‘local food’ has become fashionable in the UK without any real understanding of the issues involved. First and foremost, it is necessary to define more precisely what is meant by ‘local’. Is bread bought in England from an English bakery ‘local’ if the wheat it is made from was grown in Canada? Funded through the Rural Economy and Land Use Programme, Edwards Jones is studying people’s perceptions of ‘localness’ and seeking to establish whether there is any science behind the popular belief that ‘local is better’.


D 


His work involves studying the carbon footprint* of foods grown in different areas of the UK, as well as such common sources of supply as Spain, Kenya and Uganda. He has made some surprising discoveries. Which has the lower carbon footprint: sugar made from sugar cane grown in Africa, or from sugar beet flown in from Europe? The answer is sugar from sugar cane in Africa. Similarly, trucking vegetables in from Spain may have a smaller carbon footprint than growing them locally in the UK – because growing them locally requires adding all the emissions of running a heated greenhouse.


E 


And, he asks, how far down the life cycle should you go? He discovered early in his research that the methods used to prepare foods to be eaten can have a huge impact; boiling potatoes accounts for fully half their carbon footprint. Ultimately, it’s a mistake to look at just one part of the food chain. You could have a policy where you’re going to really pressurise farmers to try to get emissions down, but decarbonising fuel and electricity is a much more effective method of protecting the environment; he says.


F 


Another of Edwards Jones’s research projects involved visiting farm workers in each of the above countries to assess their health and well-being. ‘We found that farm workers in Kenya had better physical and mental health than the average Kenyan,’ he says, attributing the difference to both better income and to the benefits – housing, schools, medical care provided by their large corporate employers. He finds it ironic that after years of ‘trade not aid’ all of a sudden people are starting to say the UK shouldn’t be importing food from Africa.


G 


The other problem with insisting on locally grown food, he says, is that the UK is not suited to growing most fruits and vegetables – the key elements in a healthy diet. These crops need our best land – which means demoting other crops that do grow well in the UK to lower quality land. ‘There’s a domino effect so that increasing self sufficiency may, from an environmental perspective, be quite bad,’ he says.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 8


Complete the summary. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

1

The problem of world hunger

CAUSE

Some scientists see world hunger as due to a general of food.

But it may be because：

正确答案： 1 scarcity
Question 1

审题: 题目询问导致全球饥饿的原因。


定位关键信息: “world hunger” 和“due to”


对应原文: A段，“The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations notes that domestic prices of staple food are continuing to increase, leading to a rise in the number of people worldwide who are chronically underfed. According to Erik Millstone, a food and agriculture researcher funded by the Economic and Social Research Council (ESRC), the natural science community tends to view world hunger as if it were a problem that could be solved simply by increasing total production. ”


解题思路: 这句话说明有些科学家认为世界饥饿可以通过增加食物产量的方法来解决，那么就可以说食物不足是导致全球饥饿的原因，并且下文也提到了表示短缺的“scarcity”一词。

2

• hungry people cannot 

正确答案： 2 afford
Question 2

审题: 题目询问导致饥饿的另一个原因是人们不能做什么。


定位关键信息: “cannot”和“hungry people”


对应原文: A段，“Most people who are chronically hungry are so not because of the scarcity of food but because they are unable to afford what is available.”


解题思路: 原文提到埃里克·米尔斯通 (Erik Millstone) 认为大多数长期挨饿的人并不是因为食物短缺，而是因为他们买不起现有的食物。

3

• food is not available because it has been


正确答案： 3 exported
Question 3

审题: 题目询问食物无法被获得的原因。


定位关键信息: “not available”


对应原文: A段，“ In addition, the food trade is so globalised that food is often exported from areas where people are hungry and sent to countries where people already have sufficient.”


解题思路: 原文提到粮食贸易全球化导致食物经常从饥饿地区出口到已经足够的人口稠密地区。原文说明食物被出口到其他国家是食物无法被获得的原因。

4

SOLUTION

• provide support for subsistence farmers in growing and their food

正确答案： 4 storing
Question 4

审题: 题目要求填补如何帮助自给自足农民的问题。


定位关键信息: “support for subsistence farmers”


对应原文: B段，“And that is not enough on its own – they also have to have facilities for storing it so that their food can be kept safe and in good condition until it is needed. ”


解题思路: 原文提到米尔斯通认为解决问题的办法是改变贫困自给型农民的条件，并提供他们种植更多食物所需的支助，还需要储存设施来保持食物的安全和状态。

5

ADDITIONAL RECOMMENDATIONS 

• don't use to increase food production, e.g. varieties of maize are better for poor farmers as the seed can be replanted

正确答案： 5 technology  |  6 traditional
Question 5

审题: 题目问到不要用什么来增加食物产量。


定位关键信息: “increase food production”


对应原文: B段，“But, he adds, increasing productivity through technology is not the answer.”


解题思路: 原文提到增加生产力并不是答案，例如转基因高性能玉米新品种需要每年购买新种子，而贫困农民买不起。原文明确提到不应该通过技术提高生产力。


同义替换：food production = productivity

Question 6

审题: 题目问到哪种品种的玉米更适合贫穷农民。


定位关键信息: “varieties of maize”。


对应原文: B段，“Poor farmers cannot, so they need traditional varieties whose seed can be saved and replanted.”


解题思路: 原文提到转基因高性能玉米新品种的缺点是如果留种来年种植，其活力会下降，所以贫困农民更需要传统玉米种子。

7

• don't provide opportunities for farmers to have  – too risky

• don't do research into intensifying commercial farming

正确答案： 7 credit
Question 7

审题: 题目问到不要给农民提供什么机会，因为风险太大。


定位关键信息: don’t provide opportunities, too risky


对应原文: B段，“Giving farmers access to credit also doesn’t help; it adds risk.”


解题思路: 原文提到给农民提供信贷渠道会增加风险，贫困农民不是寻求投资的企业家，而是寻求降低风险的人。原文明确提到给予信贷会增加风险。

8

• do improve techniques and available to subsistence farmers

正确答案： 8 tools
Question 8

审题: 题目问到应该改善哪些资源来帮助自给自足农民。


定位关键信息: 是“improve techniques and”和“subsistence farmers”


对应原文: B段，“Ultimately, he concludes, ‘Instead of devoting resources to research for intensifying commercial farming, we should devote them to enhancing the techniques available to subsistence farmers, and to developing appropriate tools for them to use, because their need is the greatest.”

解题思路: 原文提到应该把资源用于改善可供农民使用的技术并为他们开发合适的工具，因为他们的需求最大。

Questions 9 - 13


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

9

Importing sugar to the UK from other parts of Europe is less environmentally harmful than importing sugar from Africa.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题: 题目问到从欧洲进口糖是否比从非洲进口更环保。


定位关键信息: “Importing sugar to the UK”和“environmentally harmful”。


对应原文: D段，“Which has the lower carbon footprint: sugar made from sugar cane grown in Africa, or from sugar beet flown in from Europe? The answer is sugar from sugar cane in Africa.”


解题思路: 原文指出从非洲进口糖的碳足迹较小。

10

The way in which some foods are cooked may affect their impact on the environment.

正确答案： A. TURE

审题: 题目问到食物的烹饪方式是否会影响其对环境的影响。


定位关键信息:“the way in which some foods are cooked”和“impact on the environment”。


对应原文: E段，“He discovered early in his research that the methods used to prepare foods to be eaten can have a huge impact; boiling potatoes accounts for fully half their carbon footprint.”


解题思路: 原文提到煮土豆会产生大量碳足迹，烹饪方式确实影响环境。

11

The best way to reduce harm to the environment is to oblige farmers to use more environmentally friendly farming methods.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题: 题目问到是否强迫农民使用更环保的农业方法是减少环境破坏的最好方法。


定位关键信息: 关键词是“the best way”和“reduce harm to the environment”。


对应原文: E段，“You could have a policy where you’re going to really pressurise farmers to try to get emissions down, but decarbonising fuel and electricity is a much more effective method of protecting the environment; he says.”


解题思路: 原文提到通过减少燃料和电力中的碳排放比迫使农民使用环保方法更有效，所以，强迫农民 使用更环保的农业方法并不是最好的方法。

12

Imports of food to the UK from African countries such as Kenya have fallen recently.


正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题: 题目问到最近从非洲进口的食品是否减少。


定位关键信息: “Imports of food from African countries”


对应原文：F段, “He finds it ironic that after years of ‘trade not aid’ all of a sudden people are starting to say the UK shouldn’t be importing food from Africa.”


解题思路: 原文讨论了人们对从非洲进口食品的看法转变，但没有提到进口量是否下降。

13

Growing fruit and vegetables is better for the environment than raising animals for food.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题: 题目问到种植水果和蔬菜是否比养殖动物更环保。


定位关键信息: “Growing fruit and vegetables”和“raising animals”


对应原文：G段, “The other problem with insisting on locally grown food, he says, is that the UK is not suited to growing most fruits and vegetables – the key elements in a healthy diet. These crops need our best land – which means demoting other crops that do grow well in the UK to lower quality land. ”


解题思路: G段讨论了水果和蔬菜的种植，但没有明确比较它们与动物养殖对环境的影响。
